content.claim: Micro-level evidence from Bavaria indicates poor predictive validity of age-10 selection: only 4-5% of students in the Gymnasium achieved better results in German or Math than students in the middle school, and approximately half of students deemed ineligible for Gymnasium based on 4th-grade grades had test results matching Gymnasium students.
content.context: Fresh live research through the International comparison lens
limitations: This is a single-source claim from one German state and should not be generalized cross-nationally without replication.
The finding is illustrative of selection-accuracy problems but does not directly measure whether delayed selection would improve prediction or outcomes in Hungary.
No equivalent Hungarian predictive-validity data is cited, limiting direct applicability.
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
